data.title: Foundry Opens New Outpatient Treatment Center in RiNo, Expanding Access to Trauma-Integrated Addiction Care in Downtown Denver
data.description: Denver, on April 27, 2026 – Foundry Outpatient Denver, a division of NRT Behavioral Health, has unveiled the debut of its latest outpatient treatment facility in Denver’s RiNo (River North) Art District. This center is purpose-built to cater to the increasing demand for adaptable and top-tier care services.

data.title: In HelloNation, Pavement Expert Stephen Carozza Explains Risks of Skipping Sealcoating
data.description: In Pittsford, N.Y. on April 27, 2026, it was discussed the consequences of skipping sealcoating on driveways. Many homeowners overlook this as merely a cosmetic procedure rather than an essential measure in preserving their driveway. According to Stephen Carozza from the Bedford Paving Company, highlighted in HelloNation, failing to sealcoat can lead to increased wear and tear on the driveway and expensive repairs.

data.title: In HelloNation, Towing Experts Chris and Misty Hulsey Share What to Know Before Hiring for Heavy-Duty Towing
data.description: Gainesville, Georgia, on April 27, 2026 – The distinction between heavy-duty towing and standard towing is a crucial but often misunderstood aspect for drivers and fleet operators. The specialized nature of heavy-duty towing becomes evident when the need for it arises. In a recent article by HelloNation, Towing Experts Chris and Misty Hulsey shed light on this topic. They highlighted the unique challenges and requirements involved in heavy-duty towing, emphasizing the expertise and equipment necessary to handle larger vehicles and loads. This valuable insight aims to educate individuals on the intricacies of heavy-duty towing, guiding them on the appropriate solutions when faced with such situations.

data.title: Siemens Government Technologies to Provide Electronic Security System Maintenance & Support for National Security Operations on Florida’s Space Coast
data.description: Siemens Government Technologies (SGT) has secured a competitive contract win to provide electronic security maintenance and support for national security operations at Cape Canaveral, FL. The U.S. Army Engineering and Support Center, Huntsville awarded the new task order, which could be worth up to $22.6 million if both option years are exercised. SGT will be responsible for maintaining and servicing integrated Electronic Security Systems as part of this contract.

data.title: OpenEvidence Collaborates With NCCN to Integrate Canonical Oncology Treatment Algorithms at the Point-of-Care
data.description: In Miami, OpenEvidence has announced the integration of National Comprehensive Cancer Network® (NCCN®) Clinical Practice Guidelines in Oncology (NCCN Guidelines®) into their platform. This collaboration aims to provide clinicians with a valuable resource for oncology care. The platform will now offer direct access to the NCCN Guidelines®, which serve as a crucial reference for oncology practices. This integration is part of OpenEvidence's effort to enhance their services, with a particular focus on oncology. The collaboration with NCCN will involve working with top oncology experts to ensure clinicians can deliver optimal care to patients.

data.title: Forrester: The GTM Singularity Is Collapsing Traditional Go-To-Market Approaches
data.description: In a joint announcement from Phoenix and Cambridge, the latest research from Forrester (Nasdaq: FORR) has been revealed at the B2B Summit North America. Titled "The GTM Singularity Is Here," this study serves as a wake-up call for B2B executives in marketing, sales, customer success, and product roles. It urges them to abandon outdated go-to-market (GTM) strategies and adopt a new ARC (augmented, resilient, and collaborative) GTM model that resonates with modern AI-empowered consumers. This shift comes after enduring years of turbulence influenced by evolving market trends.

data.title: OKI Develops 180-Layer 15 mm-Thick PCB Technologies for Next-Generation AI Semiconductor Testing Equipment
data.description: In Tokyo, OKI Circuit Technology, a subsidiary of the OKI Group specialized in printed circuit boards (PCBs), has announced a breakthrough in technology development. The company has unveiled innovative design and production techniques for 180-layer, 15 mm-thick PCBs designed for high bandwidth memory (HBM) used in AI semiconductors. This advancement signifies a significant boost, with a 45% surge in layer count compared to previous technology, and the board thickness has approximately doubled. The new PCBs are tailored for wafer testing equipment critical in modern semiconductor technologies.

data.title: Mercury Receives OCC Conditional Approval to Establish Mercury Bank, N.A.
data.description: Mercury, a tech company offering innovative banking solutions, has been granted conditional approval by the OCC to establish Mercury Bank, N.A. This national bank will enable Mercury to provide its unique banking services directly to customers under federal oversight. The approval comes after significant effort, signaling a major milestone for the company's vision. Through Mercury Bank, customers can expect enhanced banking experiences with full regulatory compliance.

data.title: Perini Management Services, Inc. Awarded the Right to Compete for Task Orders Under $2 Billion USACE Energy Resilience and Conservation Investment Program
data.description: In FRAMINGHAM, Massachusetts, Tutor Perini Corporation (NYSE: TPC) disclosed that its subsidiary, Perini Management Services, Inc. (PMSI), has secured a construction multiple-award task order contract (MATOC) from the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers, Louisville District. The contract spans over 10 years, including a three-year base period and seven one-year optional extensions, with a combined capacity of $2 billion. Tutor Perini Corporation is a prominent construction firm specializing in civil, building, and specialty construction projects.
